article_meta.title: News Law & the Courts Black Lives Matter Director Spent Millions in Donations on Homes, Shopping, Vacations, Indictment

reasoning: The model correctly classifies this National Review article as Lean Right with good confidence. The reasoning identifies legitimate author prose choices that tilt the coverage rightward. The key_indicators mostly cite author prose, though some are borderline factual statements from filings. Minor issues but overall solid analysis.

custom_reasoning.classification_accuracy: National Review is rated Right by AllSides, and this article from their platform carries editorial choices consistent with Lean Right to Right. The model's Lean Right classification is within one adjacent class of the likely Right actual lean, making this acceptable.
custom_reasoning.confidence_calibration: 0.85 confidence is reasonable for an article with clear but not extreme directional signals. The bias is structural (source selection, omission of balancing context, aggregation of negative BLM stories) rather than through overtly loaded language, so 0.85 is appropriate.
custom_reasoning.reasoning_evidence_quality: The key indicators mostly cite the author's own prose. 'Setting off a spending spree among leaders...' is clearly the author's editorial framing. 'Only one third of the organization's $90 million in revenue was used to fund the charity' is the author's characterization of tax filings. The Cullors and Cannon-Grant references are the author's connecting prose choices. However, some indicators are borderline - they describe factual content from filings rather than clearly loaded language. No clear attribution violations though.
